text.title: Applying VUI Principles with Agents
text.summary: Recommended agentic workflow for applying the three VUI principles: copywriter + critic subagent split, prompt patterns, and agent gotchas.

rule.statement: Split VUI generation into a copywriter agent (produces utterances) and a critic agent (scores against the rubric); never merge into one call.
rule.rationale: A single agent producing and evaluating its own output cannot reliably self-critique; the split enables measurable improvement and independent retraining.

rule.statement: Co-author every system utterance with a TTS preview; text that reads fine often sounds wrong due to prosody, pacing, or brand-name mispronunciation.
rule.rationale: Latency between user end-of-speech and first audio matters more for perceived naturalness than utterance length; the TTS preview catches both.

example.note: VUI copywriter prompt with bounded constraints
example.code: You are vui-copywriter. Rewrite the system response below to satisfy: (1) Simplicity — drop redundant phrasing, units, timestamps unless asked. (2) Naturalness — sound like a person, offer one helpful follow-up. (3) Context — refer back to <last_user_turn> implicitly, do not restate it. Max 120 characters. Ban: "I'd be happy to", "Just to confirm", "I have successfully". Return JSON: {"utterance": "...", "rationale": "...", "removed_words": N}.
Emit a confidence score for the inferred intent. If confidence < 0.7, route to a clarification agent instead of generating a response.

antipattern.description: LLM generates verbose, apologetic responses despite instructions.
antipattern.reason: Add a stop-list to the system prompt and enforce max word count in post-processing — models do not reliably self-edit on tone alone.

antipattern.description: Context-awareness regressions are silent — the agent answers but ignores the prior turn.
antipattern.reason: Always include the last 3-5 turns verbatim in the prompt and assert the response references at least one entity from them in the critic pass.

antipattern.description: LLM counts words or characters to enforce length limits.
antipattern.reason: LLMs are unreliable at counting; enforce length limits in code post-generation, not via model instruction.

antipattern.description: Single agent decides both "is auth required?" and "speak the response" for sensitive operations.
antipattern.reason: Split into a policy agent and a response agent; the executor must never see raw sensitive values. See vui-privacy-security methodology for the full pattern.

text.reference: Linter flagging over-long, multi-clause, or filler-filled utterances.
System prompt for the VUI copywriter subagent.
